---
title: "Spatial Point Processes: Theory and Applications"
subtitle: "Track B — Geospatial"
date: "2025-01-01"
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
number-sections: true
---

# Spatial Point Processes: Theory and Applications

**Track**: Track B — Geospatial

The mathematical theory of random collections of points in space. Point processes model event locations: disease cases, tree positions, earthquake epicenters, crime incidents, and neural spike locations.

## Table of Contents

1. Fundamentals of Point Processes
2. Poisson Processes on General Spaces
3. Summary Statistics: K-Function and Beyond
4. Cox Processes and Log-Gaussian Cox Processes
5. Gibbs and Markov Point Processes
6. Marked Point Processes
7. Spatio-Temporal Point Processes
8. Point Process Regression and Modern Methods

---

## Chapter 1: Fundamentals of Point Processes

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 2: Poisson Processes on General Spaces

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 3: Summary Statistics: K-Function and Beyond

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 4: Cox Processes and Log-Gaussian Cox Processes

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 5: Gibbs and Markov Point Processes

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 6: Marked Point Processes

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 7: Spatio-Temporal Point Processes

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 8: Point Process Regression and Modern Methods

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*
